title: Effects of listening to Evangelions OST
display_title: Effects of listening to Evangelions OST
wikitext:
[]: Effects of listening to Evangelion's OST             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:58 am I've always wonder, what happens to people when they listen to Evangelion's OST's and BGM? As for me, I get lots of flashbacks and I always see a mental image of the entry plug in my mind. Weird right? How 'bout you?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sun Nov 08, 2009 11:18 am Isn't this chit-chat'ish material? :p Anyway, unfortunately NGE's music isn't that amazing, and isn't something that I would listen to outside of when it is played in the series. It does its job to set the mood in the series, though... Anyway regarding your question and keeping in mind the only osts I listen to are the beast II, normal blood, thanatos and its rhythm only version. I have a subconscious connection with the scenes they were played in so I either feel, perhaps, excited when listening to the beast2/normal blood, and thanatos just gives me shivers, and the rhythm version gives me the strongest feelings with flashbacks to Misato/Shinji elevator scene, Its a very sad song but beautiful nonetheless.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 08, 2009 4:24 pm S2 Works as least is good work music for me when I'm doing whatever, so I tend to use it that way. As for what I think about when listening to it, lately I wonder what pieces would fit best in this Rebuild visual novel thing of mine, but other then that I think of scenes from the series or wonder how the later psychological pieces from the series will be changed/tweaked for 3.0/4.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sun Nov 08, 2009 4:59 pm The OST might not have the depth and complexity of Howard Shore, but it has its own touch, in that it's unmistakenly Evangelion. Thanatos is sorta cheesy, but any other piece dealing with Rei is incredible.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:13 pm I find myself having goosebumps whenever I listen to Kyou no Hi wa Sayonara and Tsubasa wo Kudasai. Angel of Doom on the other hand gives me a sense of superiority everytime I play Counter-Strike. I find it comforting everytime I listen to Thanathos ~If I Can't Be Yours~, Thanathos ~instrumental~, Beautiful World ~PLANiTb Acoustica Remix~ and Beautiful world. S^2 Works makes me sleep soundly, keeps me calm. I like the Hallelujah chorus and Thanathos both played by the Japanese Philharmonic Orchestra. I forgot to mention Air which was played in EoE. I always get the mental image of Asuka fighting the MP Evas, epic!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:16 pm There are a couple of tracks that I like, but for the most part I think that the music of Evangelion is really terrible.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:19 pm I like Canon, Both Thanatos(es) the instrumental makes me think of a happy Shinji overcomes his issues and defeats the MP Evas, even when thats the furthest thing from when the music is played (It just has victorious or triumphant vibe to it ), You have GOT TO love Cruel Angel's Thesis, Soul's Refrain seems to get me pumped, and I also enjoy the 3 mixes of Fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:26 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: I like Canon, Both Thanatos(es) the instrumental makes me think of a happy Shinji overcomes his issues and defeats the MP Evas, even when thats the furthest thing from when the music is played (It just has victorious or triumphant vibe to it ), You have GOT TO love Cruel Angel's Thesis, Soul's Refrain seems to get me pumped, and I also enjoy the 3 mixes of Fly me to the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Nov 08, 2009 9:34 pm I was thinking more of the upbeat/uptempoed parts, I cant describe it in words, but its like in the middle and/or end of the piece. Soul's Refrain has that epic final battle vibe to it IMO, Beautiful World is also good. And despite of the situation Give Me Wings also gives me possitive warm kind of feeling. The Rebuild Bardiel song ( Dont know the name ) however DOES make me sad when listening to it. I also enjoy Expansion of Blockage, TBH I think it actually has a more of triumphant feeling then Thanatos, because when its played Shinji actually IS overcoming part of his problems somewhat and deciding he wants to live in reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Sun Nov 08, 2009 11:01 pm I've always been partial to this one.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Sun Nov 08, 2009 11:30 pm I downloaded a bunch of tracks and put together a playlist which I've had running on WinAmp as I type my NaNo novel (though since this fic is sort of an "extended universe"-type story, I've added in a few other tracks from other series as well). It's definitely helping me keep in the spirit of the series...

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Nov 09, 2009 2:21 am It was sadly one of my only friends in college. My college was about 2 miles away from my dorm and I had to walk though the city by myself all year round. I saw people from my class drive by and look at me and never offer a ride but the music at least kept me happy. It only fit that I would have a white dress shirt on most of the time since we had to dress nice for school and the NERV bag only made it better I guess....What was I getting at again?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Nov 09, 2009 7:57 am This is a nice AMV for Asuka likers/Soul's refrain listeners.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:08 pm I'm not really fond of the soundtrack. In other words I wouldn't listen to most of pieces casually. There are few songs that I really enjoy ( Good, or Don't Be , Kom Susser Todd , and The Heavy Feeling of Freedom come to mind) that I like to listen to when I feel like it.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:17 pm Komm Susser Todd is good too, but then most of us would rather listen to Hey Jude.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:26 pm I have 5 hours of eva music, six and and a half if you count rebuild stuff that I don't listen to. I love listening to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:31 pm I like Rebuild's soundtrack more than the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:37 pm I can't believe nobody mentioned Infantile Dependence, Adult Dependency yet! A piece so obsessive and intense that was just perfect for the ending of Episode 25. I can't help but repeat all the ending lines of that episode each time I listen to it. Other than that, The Heady Feeling of Freedom and Good, or Don't Be from the ending of 26 are pretty good. I just have to smile listening to them. Congratulations!

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:47 pm ath wrote: I can't believe nobody mentioned Infantile Dependence, Adult Dependency yet! A piece so obsessive and intense that was just perfect for the ending of Episode 25. I can't help but repeat all the ending lines of that episode each time I listen to it.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:59 pm slothen wrote: do you know what the moonspeak names are for these tracks?
